title: Quaternionic Logic: A G-Lattice Unifying Boolean and Fuzzy Frameworks
description: A formal logic defined as a G-lattice: a bounded residuated lattice on [0,1] x [-1,1]^3 equipped with an SU(2) group action by quaternionic conjugation. Connectives (AND, OR, NOT, IMPLIES) define what is true; conjugation q*a*q^{-1} defines from which perspective one evaluates. Central theorem: connectives commute with context transformations if and only if they operate only on the real component. Corollary: Boolean logic is the context-free special case; full quaternionic logic is context-dependent. Six known logics (Boolean, Godel fuzzy, Godel-Kripke modal, Kleene trivalent, ordinal, probabilistic) are recovered as dimensional restrictions. Soundness and completeness are proved via reduction to Godel logic with involutive negation. No exact precedent for this combination (residuated lattice + SU(2) conjugation with real-part invariance) was found in the literature.
